trajectory.description: Observation: baseline pipeline almost worked; failure is a simple API misuse because TSR has no model.device attribute. Analysis: the documented API says call model([pil_image], device=device), so predict.py should track the torch device explicitly. We should also make local smoke test robust and immediately submit eval after fixing, per the required early-baseline workflow. Decision: patch predict.py to pass device through render_object, keep offline loading, run a quick local prediction test on val data, then submit the existing checkpoint for evaluation.

Observation: direct TripoSR baseline is valid and gives LPIPS 0.6191, but metrics are extremely poor, indicating the built-in render camera sequence does not match dataset cameras. Analysis: the main bottleneck is camera mismatch, not training. Since TripoSR API docs only expose render(n_views) and not custom camera poses, a practical improvement is to learn a view-synthesis model that maps the input image plus target camera delta to the target view directly. With only 800 train objects and 11 targets each, a lightweight conditional UNet can be trained quickly and should beat the mismatched baseline. Decision: implement a camera-conditioned image-to-image model, train for a few epochs to get a fast second baseline, test local prediction, and keep predict.py compatible with both learned model and fallback TripoSR.

trajectory.description: Observation: TripoSR baseline is valid but LPIPS 0.6191 is very poor, confirming camera mismatch dominates. Analysis: the best next move is a lightweight camera-conditioned direct novel-view synthesis model trained on the provided paired data; this avoids dependence on undocumented TripoSR custom-camera APIs and should train quickly within budget. Decision: replace train.py/predict.py with a compact conditional encoder-decoder that takes input RGBA plus target camera delta features, train for a few epochs with validation and checkpointing, then run a local prediction smoke test.

Observation: the camera-conditioned UNet trained successfully in ~2 minutes and local prediction works, but we still have no eval score for this new model. Analysis: per workflow, the highest-value next step is to submit the new checkpoint immediately to measure whether direct camera-conditioned synthesis beats the poor TripoSR baseline; only then should we decide whether to scale training or change architecture. Decision: submit checkpoints/cam_unet.pt with the current predict.py for evaluation now.

trajectory.description: Observation: the camera-conditioned UNet improved LPIPS substantially from 0.6191 to 0.3922, so the direct novel-view synthesis approach is working. Analysis: current model is likely underpowered and trained with only pixel losses at 128px; the next high-value improvement is to increase capacity modestly, add a perceptual loss using pretrained ResNet features, and train longer while staying within the fast iteration budget. Decision: upgrade model width, add multi-scale skip refinement and perceptual loss in train.py, keep predict.py backward-compatible, train a stronger checkpoint for more epochs, then evaluate it.

Observation: offline LPIPS training succeeded and produced a new checkpoint after a long but acceptable run (~10.6 min), with validation loss dropping to 0.0703. Analysis: the most valuable next step is to evaluate this new checkpoint immediately before spending more iterations, since the previous best eval is 0.3922 and we need to know whether the stronger model actually improves LPIPS on hidden test. Decision: submit checkpoints/cam_unet_v2.pt with the current predict.py now.

trajectory.description: Observation: cam_unet_v2 improved hidden-test LPIPS from 0.3922 to 0.3409, a strong gain, and training took about 10.6 minutes with stable loss around 0.07. Analysis: the current bottleneck is likely limited output sharpness/detail from pure direct regression at 128px. A high-value next step that stays incremental is to train longer with a slightly larger model and add a simple edge-aware loss on composited RGB, while keeping the same proven pipeline and predict contract. This should improve perceptual fidelity without risky architectural changes or dependency issues. Decision: patch train.py to add Sobel edge loss and optional resume support, train a larger/longer cam_unet_v3 from scratch for 20 epochs at 128px, then evaluate the new checkpoint immediately.

Observation: cam_unet_v3 trained successfully for ~19.5 minutes and reached validation loss 0.070852, close to but slightly worse than the prior v2 best validation (~0.0703), so improvement is uncertain. Analysis: the highest-value action now is immediate hidden-set evaluation of the new checkpoint before spending more iterations; if it improves LPIPS we keep it, if not we revert to v2. Decision: submit checkpoints/cam_unet_v3.pt with the current predict.py now.

Analysis: the old v3 checkpoint was trained with the earlier architecture (inc/down/up naming), while current model.py defines a different architecture (in_proj/down1/down2...).
